Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(3 inline comments)

Also, patch needs rebase.  build/autoMakefile.am.toplevel no longer exists in master.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

This isn't right, I built the DKMS package and the filename contains the kernel version:

    lustre-client-modules-dkms-2.3.56-2.6.32_279.9.1.1chaos.ch5.1.x86_64_g6a7cb4c.noarch.rpm

The DKMS package should be independent of any specific kernel.
Patch Set 1: (4 inline comments)

Patch Set 1:

I agree the naming of RPM is not as simple as it should be, but since builded as a sub-package I don't see how to change this right now. Even the "-n" option for sub-package will not allow to not inherit the kernel version in the RPM name, I think. Any idea is welcome.

Patch Set 1: (2 inline comments)

To get a different version, you need to have a separate "Version:" line.  Possibly also "Release:".  I've been avoiding it, but it is probably time to take a hard look at the way version numbers are generated in Lustre.

Patch Set 1:

Wow, it is so simple, thank's Chris. I spent sometimes to read RPM documentations and also looking into multiple spec file examples, but I did not find this. BTW, Release: is required and thus I set it to 1 like lustre-iokit ...
Uploaded patch set 2.

http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20497/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 9:

(2 comments)

It's too bad so much of the build steps in jenkins are in jenkins rather than just having jenkins' build steps be a one-liner to just run the steps from the tree.  Then you could actually include building the dkms modules as part of the build, verifying the work in this patch actually works,

Patch Set 9:

Although, given that this is actually DKMS module building, trying to build it within a jenkins job is futile since the whole point of DKMS modules are to be able to install their RPM and have them built with the current kernel.

I wonder if DKMS has some sort of no-root-required way to do the build when pointed at the modules and source tree, etc. like we do with lustre's configure -- after unpacking some rpms with cpio etc.

Anyway, just some rambling thoughts on how we can actually be testing this through our build/test cycle.  Maybe some tests need to be added to the test framework.

Patch Set 9:

Brian, [auto-]tests of DKMS generated RPMS is an issue that I raised to the Tools team with TT-1112, then duped by TT-1244/TEI-74 tickets. Seems that going thru this TT-/TEI- tickets serie there have been some deviation from original purpose, that was howto to handle/integrate Lustre DKMS packages testing, to zfs/zpl only focus but I think we can raise it again …

Patch Set 9:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/2ae9b12c-6da7-11e3-af30-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9: Verified+1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/1dbab138-6df0-11e3-bae0-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 9:

TEI-1359 has been created, as a follow-on to TT-1112 in order to have DKMS RPMs testing become part of autotests. Because even if this testing could mimic'ed by some mean, DKMS RPMs install/build needs to be tested in its main/admin environment and purpose.

Patch Set 9:

Brian, I am not sure to understand your comment about futility of building DKMS RPMs as part of a Jenkins build …. Can you detail this and provide some details about what could be better done ?? Thanks.

Patch Set 9:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 9:

Bruno, I think my comments on DKMS modules and futility were more related to testing them, by using them in our test workflow.  But I forgot that you guys use autotest for that, where you will be root and can install DKMS packages and have them built and used for your tests.  I was thinking it was Jenkins that was going to have to be able to install them to test them, which it cannot do of course, not being root.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

How we will handle our DKMS modules testing looks like a very tricky subject to me and this is why I again would like to get people of our Test-Team involved with TEI-1359.
Uploaded patch set 10.

Since none of CI will actually test this, it would be prudent for you to test on your own development system and add a +1 Verified that it all works for you locally, at least.

If you dead set against that, can you please explain the reasons why you felt this was a better approach.  Because I'm just not seeing it.

parents.subject: LU-4379 procfs: dont always check max_pages_per_rpc alignement

commit.subject: LU-1032 build: Add DKMS support client kernel modules.
commit.message:
LU-1032 build: Add DKMS support client kernel modules.

This patch permits the creation of a new RPM DKMS-aware.
It will allow for Lustre-Client modules re-build within DKMS
infrastructure.
Lustre-Server modules future re-build has been somewhat planned.
Further work may be needed (scripted?) to handle llite_lloop and
ptlrpd_gss special cases, due to their respective configure-time
and gss/krb5 dependencies on target platforms.

DKMS is a cross distro mechanism for the building and maintenance
of out-of-tree kernel modules. (http://linux.dell.com/dkms/)

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: I8dfb7bbbae6da8a9c06fc603791c94642b3669cf
